content: Eating every two or three hours is considered a flat-out myth for boosting metabolism.
When we eat constantly, our bodies do not have the opportunity to utilize stored energy sources like body fat.
Eating frequently leads to constant insulin secretion, which inhibits fat burning and slows enzyme activity.
Insulin is secreted by the pancreas regardless of whether carbohydrates or proteins are consumed.
Elevated insulin levels can cause the pancreas to become lazy and less efficient over time, potentially leading to diabetes.
The thermic effect of food is not increased by eating frequently; it depends on the quantity of food consumed.
